core_mechanism: Bayesian model-based inference of an expectation over a sample space partitioned into equivalence classes, replacing a direct ratio/sample-average estimator: samples are binned by group-conjugacy class, a parametric baseline is fit under informative priors that pin its known asymptotic limits, a learned residual corrects the baseline, and the target quantity is inferred from the fitted class-wise models rather than averaged from raw samples, with adaptive importance sampling steering draws toward undersampled classes to reduce variance
brief_reason: The abstract recasts the arithmetic calculation of an observable as a statistical inference problem, binning exchange cycles by permutation conjugacy class, fitting Bayesian priors that enforce asymptotic-limit models plus an LSTM residual, and inferring the result from these models instead of a direct ratio estimator, with active importance sampling toward undersampled regions.
